figures.caption: Fig. 1: The Fw β scores with respect to the trainable parameters of different methods on COD10K (RGB-Depth), PCOD-1200 (RGB-Polarization), and VIAC (RGB-Thermal) datasets.
TABLE III: Quantitative comparisons with SOTAs on the VIAC (RGB-T) dataset. The best results are highlighted in bold. * represents the SAM-based methods.
Fig. 3: Qualitative comparison of detection results on RGB-D and RGB-P camouflaged object detection datasets.
TABLE IV: Unified ablation study under standard and joint training settings.

Fig. 2: Overview of our proposed framework. The framework adopts a dual-stream architecture where RGB and auxiliary modalities are processed via a frozen SAM image encoder and a PVT encoder, respectively, to construct the content domain through feature summation. In Content-Prompt Interaction Module (CPIM), a set of learnable parameters serves as the prompt domain prototype, interacting with the content domain to achieve synergy between data-driven and knowledge-driven representations. Finally, the enhanced content features, along with learnable auxi-tokens and SAM’s original tokens, are fed into the mask decoder, followed by a Mask Refine Module (MRM) to produce the final precise segmentation.
Fig. 8: Comparison under different challenging scenarios.
TABLE I: Comparison of SOTA methods on multiple RGB-Depth datasets in terms of Sα, Fw β , Eϕ, and M . The best results are highlighted in bold. * represents the SAM-based methods.
TABLE II: Quantitative comparisons with SOTAs on PCOD1200. The best results are highlighted in bold. * represents the SAM-based methods.
